body:
In Alameda County and the broader San Francisco Bay Area, the cost to replace garage door springs typically ranges from **$350 to $750 total** , including both parts and professional labor.

Because the Bay Area has a higher cost of living and higher labor rates compared to the national average, prices sit toward the higher end of standard estimates.

## Cost Breakdown by Spring Type

Most residential garage doors use two springs. Even if only one breaks, technicians strongly recommend replacing both at the same time so the door stays properly balanced. [How Much Does It Cost to Replace a Garage Door Spring? (2025 Data) - HomeAdvisor](https://www.homeadvisor.com/cost/garages/garage-door-spring-repair/#:~:text=Torsion%20spring%20replacement%20costs%20anywhere,both%20at%20the%20same%20time.)

Service / Spring Type| Estimated Cost Range (Bay Area)  
---|---  
**Dual Extension Springs** (stretch along the tracks)| $350 – $500  
**Dual Torsion Springs** (mounted on a bar above the door)| $450 – $750  
**Single Torsion Spring** (for light, single-car doors)| $250 – $450  
  
## Key Factors That Impact the Price

  * **Labor Rates:** Labor in Alameda County generally runs between **$100 and $200 per hour**. The job usually takes an experienced technician 1 to 1.5 hours.

  * **Trip/Service Fees:** Many local companies charge a baseline service fee (around $75 to $125) just to come out, though this is frequently waived or absorbed into the total cost if you agree to have them do the repair.

  * **Door Size and Weight:** Heavy custom wood doors or double-wide glass/steel doors require thick, heavy-duty commercial-grade springs, which can add $50 to $100 to the parts cost.

  * **The "Upsell" Factor:** Be aware that some corporate national chains operating in the East Bay are notorious for quoting a low price over the phone and then presenting a $1,500+ invoice at your house by bundling unnecessary replacements for cables, drums, and rollers. Unless those parts are visibly frayed or broken, sticking strictly to the springs should keep you in the under-$750 range.

> ⚠️ **Safety Note:** Garage door springs operate under immense mechanical tension. Attempting a DIY replacement without the proper winding bars and experience can cause severe injury. It is highly recommended to hire a licensed, insured local professional. [Garage Door Spring Replacement Cost (2026) - This Old House](https://www.thisoldhouse.com/garages/garage-door-spring-cost#:~:text=Professional%20installation%20is%20strongly%20recommended,%24540)
